pattern: Enumerate all ways to distribute k distinct-colored balls into two equal-sized halves using recursive combinatorics, tracking distinct color counts and comparing against the uniform total.
hints: For each color, decide how many balls of that color go into box 1 — enumerate all valid splits summing to n total per box using nested recursion or DP.
Weight each split by the multinomial coefficient for that color's allocation; divide the favorable weight by the total weight of all equal-size splits.
Track the number of distinct colors in each box as you recurse across colors; the condition is distinct_count(box1) == distinct_count(box2).

edge_inputs.description: One color with 2 balls — both boxes must have same color, probability 1
Two colors one ball each — both arrangements give each box 1 color
Max distinct colors each with 1 ball — 8 colors
One color dominates with max count
All balls of same color — box1 always has 1 distinct, box2 always has 1 distinct
